replies.author: @ComposedSage75
replies.text:
@ChaoticHeretic  Here’s where you’re objectively incorrect: 

- Kingsman was objectively correct in his actions and responses during the $40,000 Marvel Rivals tournament controversy. He repeatedly emphasized that he was advocating for optimal team cohesion—specifically a triple support meta strategy—to increase their chances of success in a high-stakes tournament. His concern was rooted in competitive strategy and the financial need to win, as he openly shared that he was a college student relying on the prize money for essentials.

- His suggestion to switch Zazzastack from Black Widow to a more viable pick like Hela or Winter Soldier was based on gameplay viability, not personal attacks. Multiple individuals confirmed that every other team in the tournament ultimately used triple support, validating his strategic concerns. His team finished last place with only one win, further underscoring the impact of their poor team cohesion.

- Kingsman was kicked from the tournament after the scrims, meaning the official competition was played without him. His team (with Cece, Zazzastack, and Luci) competed in the real event and finished last. Cece and her team prioritized _"vibes”_ and personal attachment to Black Widow while Kingsman’s push for a competitive strategy was reasonable and logical. Cece’s response—calling him a _“loser,”_mocking his financial situation, and using her platform to amplify harassment—was heavily criticized as unprofessional and plain wrong.

- She showed lack of accountability and poor sportsmanship. The tournament organizer, BasimZB, also faced pushback for enforcing a zero-tolerance policy without proper investigation. Kingsman’s focus on winning, his transparency about his financial needs, and the eventual validation of his strategy make him the objectively correct party overall.

If a top 1 player who had matches against top 1 and top 2 widow player giving advice and PROVED THE POINT over and over and OVER again you should know that you SHOULD listen to them. He was being respectful about it. When he went silent the losers kept bringing it back up trying to get a reaction out of him. He was right even though he was kind of annoying. He didn't deserve to be mocked at for trying to be COMPETITIVE in a COMPETITIVE GAME in a COMPETITIVE TOURNAMENT. If youre playing for the vibes don't even join the tournament,just run duos.

replies.author: @ComposedSage75
replies.text:
@ChaoticHeretic 

- Kingsman’s repeated requests were rooted in competitive strategy, not pettiness, and were supported by gameplay evidence: Zazzastack had poor ratios in scrims, while Kingsman carried the team with high wins and low losses. His offer to switch to Mantis support further demonstrated his willingness to adapt for team success. His strategic concerns were valid. CeCe’s was not. 

- CeCe dismissed valid strategic feedback, defended a non-meta pick without justification, and reacted emotionally to strategy that was logical given their circumstances in game. Her claim of a "panic attack" was used to deflect accountability, and her apology, which was heavily questionable, lacked sincerity. She also failed to mediate or compromise, instead escalating tensions.

- Kingsman’s team would not have performed significantly better without his requests, and the outcome would’ve remained the same or even worse. Kingsman’s team did not win any games after he was removed, and the team that replaced him did not advance past the semifinals, indicating that his removal didn’t lead to a better outcome. Multiple sources note that Kingsman was the primary carry, and his team’s performance relied heavily on him—especially when he was playing high-impact roles like Mantis or DPS.

- Moreover, Cece’s team had a significant rank disparity, with Kingsman and his duo, Luci, being the highest rank, while others were Grandmaster or Diamond. This imbalance shows that the team wouldn’t have been cohesive enough to win consistently. Kingsman’s requests were driven by a desire to win, and his presence was crucial. Without him, the team lost its top performer, and the results did not improve—proving that the outcome would have remained unchanged or possibly worsened. 

- While Kingsman pushed for Zazzastack to switch from Black Widow to Bucky or Phoenix, citing poor performance and ineffective team composition against dive comps, the evidence shows how the team struggled regardless. In scrim matches, Zazzastack had a 0-5 record as Widow, and Kingsman was carrying the team, often with a 20+ count compared to her 0 count.

- I acknowledged that Kingsman’s approach was assertive but it ended up proving him right seeing as the results of not listening to him ended up costing the team later on which I’ve addressed this to you twice now. You’re willfully choosing to ignore that. This was a high stakes tournament for him and them seeing as a lot of money was on the line so it makes sense to change up strategy so that all members involved could win. If what they were doing before wasn’t working to their advantage then he was right in calling it out. Take your own advice and watch his video because clearly you’re still not understanding this.

replies.author: @akira8393
replies.text: ​@ChaoticHereticis this her boyfriend alt account😂

replies.author: @ChaoticHeretic
replies.text:
@ComposedSage75  At this point I am just going to assume you actually didn't watch the Kingsman Original video, this goes for everyone else as well. It is okay, I thought the same way you do now until I actually watched his video. 

1) Kingsman makes a suggestion for Zazza to change to Winter Soldier, a character she doesn't know how to play as well as Black Widow. She declines politely. 

2) Kingsmen persist, explaining to them that it would be a better play and around this time, the three support topic is brought up.

3) Zazza goes 0/5/0 of which props Kingsman to make a deal out of it that his prior points about team comp ( However, this is later proven to not actually be accurate) They begin to argue because HE raised his voice and started making a ordeal out of it.

4) Kingsmen begins to argue with them about triple support, explaining that other teams are going to be using this strat and that they need a third support, again She refuses. She also goes 6/8 this match. Kingsmen also says he will switch to mantis but his team ask him to keep playing Magik ( IF TRIPLE SUPPORT WAS REALLY THE ONLY WAY, HE WOULD HAVE STILL DONE IT REGARDLESS. )

5) Kingsmen notices one of the players on the enemy team is someone called Snake and explains that if Zazza doesn't switch to Winter Soldier, they are going to lose the match. She doesn't switch and more arguing ensues, however, shortly after, he quiets down and they actually win the match with zazza doing almost as well as Kingsmen ( Proving him wrong.)

He tries to gaslight them by bringing up his desire for collage.

6) They then proceed to win matches with a noticeable lack of Kingsmen complaining which even Zazza herself says was hindering her play.

----

The moment they explained to him that it doesn't matter what he says, Zazza is going to play black widow he should have just decided to focus or leave. These teams aren't an esports team, they are just people thrown together for an event. Just because there is a money involved doesn't mean you are entitled to someone else's fun and or life.

It doesn't matter if what he was suggesting is competitively sound, if someone doesn't want to switch then they don't want to switch.

I hate that he got removed from the tournament the way he did but if he would have dropped the situation sooner, it wouldn't have escalated to such a point.

Aside from him justly making a video about being removed from the tournament, it also looks like his brain erupted that someone would dare defy meta and so it was very important to him to get as many people as possible to hold him so he could keep his Ego intact.

replies.author: @ComposedSage75
replies.text:
@ChaoticHeretic

- Dude, if you watched the video and are still saying he was in the wrong here just because he decided to address their behaviors towards his concerns that led up to their team losing then you didn’t pay thorough enough attention. 

- Kingsman repeatedly advocated for optimal team composition—specifically, switching his teammate Zazzastack (a one-trick Black Widow player) to a more competitive characters, citing the dominant "triple support" meta. It was a suggestion. He wasn’t being aggressive or had a hurt ego. He merely offered advice which they ignored that cost them later on. He emphasized his need for the prize money to help pay for college, framing his push as a strategic necessity, not personal hostility. You can keep claiming that he didn’t need to bring it up like what you’ve said consistently here but he chose to give that information so it was his choice to do so. If you find that manipulative or unnecessary then that’s your own business. However, that still doesn’t detract from the results that happened. 

- Cece, the team captain, and other teammates, including Luci and Zazzastack, reacted negatively, telling Kingsman to be silent while mocking his financial situation, and dismissing his concerns. Despite Kingsman carrying matches (evidenced by high counts and Zazzastack going 0–5 in one match), his teammates refused to adapt despite his proven warnings. After a dispute escalated, Zazzastack’s boyfriend compiled a Google Doc claiming Kingsman was _“toxic,”_ which was submitted to BasimZD. The tournament organizer removed Kingsman from the event arbitrarily, citing rule violations—though no clear evidence of misconduct was shown.


-Was the tournament for fun?

While initially presented as a casual event, the $40,000 prize pool was added just before the tournament, leading to accusations of bait-and-switch. Multiple sources confirm the event was marketed as fun, but with real money at stake, expectations diverged. That was more-so on the event organizers rather than your assumptions of him trying to ruin the fun or having claim to their lives which he never said at any point in the video nor was that ever hinted whatsoever. 


-Was Kingsman manipulative?

No. His actions were not manipulative. He did not insult teammates, threaten anyone, or use harassment. He consistently framed his arguments around team success and competitive integrity, offering to switch to support (Mantis) to accommodate Zazzastack’s playstyle. His motivation—using prize money for college—was repeatedly stated and verified.


-Was his team objectively gaslighting him?

Yes. Multiple sources confirm systematic gaslighting:


* Cece dismissed his strategic input as “annoying” and claimed the team had “better vibes” without him.

* She later admitted she failed to respect his wishes and called him a _”loser”_ in private messages.

* Zazzastack’s boyfriend made a fake Google Doc that was later revealed to be embellished to try and discredit him. It failed btw. 


-Was his response justified?

Absolutely. Kingsman released a detailed video explaining the situation, including direct messages and match footage. His calm, fact-based rebuttal exposed the team’s lack of accountability. He did not attack individuals personally, only the decision to remove him. His team prioritized ego and personal preference over strategy and fairness. The removal was based on emotional reactions and flawed evidence. The tournament’s outcome—his team finishing last—proved his predictions accurate regardless of how you see it. The incident highlighted the dangers of performative “fun” at the expense (ridicule) of others. 

- I’ve said more than my peace on this issue. If you disagree then so be it. I’ve given objective stances on this and I didn’t need to stoop to saying things like _”I find it hilarious how one sided you people view this”_ or that I’m _”making excuses for him”_ when you can clearly read that I kept feelings on this out and only stated the facts in question. I’m glad you proceeded to take your own advice and watched the video like most people did regarding this subject but as I’ve said it’s clear that you didn’t understand thoroughly the facts pertaining to what happened and whether or not the responses were justified. Feel free to comment further in this section if that’s what you feel like doing but I have no more reason to continue with you beyond this last comment.

Anyway, its clear you aren't actually trying to understand anything I am saying so this is pointless. I hope you are having a great morning.

replies.text: 😫 Misogynist; noun, a man (or woman) who hates women. That is what misogyny is; the hatred of women. Misogyny is not criticising a woman. It’s not offending them. It’s not saying a joke at their expense. Its not, not wanting to watch their sport. It’s not, not voting for a woman or wanting attractive women in video games. It’s not, not wanting an OF 304 woman; None of that is misogyny! Misogyny is the hatred/dislike of women, as in plural. Just like misandry is the hatred of men, almost all feminists are misandrists. And I don’t believe that there is a man who hates/dislikes all women.
